days.label: Friday
days.title: On Bleecker Street at the Bandstand
days.description: The Simon & Garfunkel tribute act takes the Rehoboth Bandstand at 8 PM. Free, all ages, and the boardwalk smells like popcorn the whole walk over.

days.label: Saturday
days.title: Echoes plays the Bandstand
days.description: Rehoboth's summer concert series keeps going with an American Pink Floyd tribute, 8 to 9:15 PM. Bring a blanket and get there by 7:30 for a spot on the grass.

days.label: Sunday
days.title: Slow paddle out of Lewes
days.description: The canal is closest to glass in the first hour after sunrise, before the wake boats start. Coffee at the Lightship after works every time.

days.label: Next Weekend
days.title: Elvis at the Bandstand, one more time
days.description: Jesse Garron's Elvis tribute hits the Rehoboth Bandstand July 24. The free concert series runs every weekend through Labor Day, so this is nowhere near the last one.

days.label: Later This Month
days.title: Bandstand season, in full swing
days.description: Rehoboth's free concerts keep the boardwalk loud on weekend nights straight through the end of summer. Worth building a whole evening around, not just a stop on the way somewhere else.

days.label: Holiday Weekend
days.title: Sea Witch Festival returns
days.description: Rehoboth's costume parade, pet costume contest and boardwalk crowds mark the unofficial end of the season. It's on the calendar early because it sells out hotels early, too.
